definition: An object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by a net force
Net force equals mass times acceleration (F = ma)
For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction
The rate of change of position; a vector with magnitude (speed) and direction
The rate of change of velocity
Mass times velocity; a conserved vector quantity in isolated systems
Energy of motion equal to one-half mass times velocity squared
Stored energy that depends on an object's position or configuration
A force that opposes relative motion between two surfaces in contact
Force applied over a distance; equal to the change in energy
